title: Administrative Assistant
how_to_become: To become an administrative assistant in the NHS, apply for vacancies on the NHS recruitment website. You could also do a Modern Apprenticeship.

salary.entry_note: Administrative assistants in the NHS start at £25,694 before tax.
salary.experienced_note: Senior administrative assistants in the NHS can earn up to £33,016 before tax.

description: In the NHS, administrative assistants work in hospitals, health centres, and board headquarters. They provide business support to clinical staff and business teams to arrange meetings, create documents, and maintain records.

entry_requirements.overview: To become an administrative assistant, you'll need a good standard of education.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience. This could help when applying to college or a new job with NHSScotland.

education_and_training.modern_apprenticeship: With a Business Administration Modern Apprenticeship, you'll get the training and experience needed and a recognised qualification.
college_courses: SVQ Business and Administration at SCQF level 5
SVQ Customer Service at SCQF level 5

role_overview: Communicating, time management, and organisation skills are key. You could work in a clinical setting or in an office providing administrative support.

learning_and_development.note: Your NHS employer will confirm mandatory training requirements during induction.

qualifications: SVQ Business and Administration at SCQF level 6
HNC Administration and Information Technology at SCQF level 7

chunks.text: To become an administrative assistant in the NHS, apply for vacancies on the NHS recruitment website. You could also do a Modern Apprenticeship.
NHS administrative assistants start at £25,694 before tax at entry level. Senior administrative assistants can earn up to £33,016 before tax.
In the NHS, administrative assistants work in hospitals, health centres, and board headquarters, providing business support to arrange meetings, create documents, and maintain records.
A good standard of education is needed. Useful subjects include Administration and IT, Business Management, English, and Maths.
Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying to college or a new job with NHSScotland.
Options include a Business Administration Modern Apprenticeship, SVQ Business and Administration at SCQF level 5, and SVQ Customer Service at SCQF level 5.
Key skills include communication, time management, and organisation. The role can be in a clinical setting like a reception desk or in an office.
Mandatory training includes health and safety, occupational health, fire safety, counter-fraud, moving and handling, and safe information handling.
Further qualifications like SVQ Business and Administration at SCQF level 6 and HNC Administration and IT at SCQF level 7 support progression into team leader or office manager roles.
